name: So Fine Key Lime Pie
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup evaporated skim milk, well chilled
10 extra-thin fresh lime slices, for garnish
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small bowl sprinkle gelatin over lime juice. Let stand until softened, 5 minutes. Stir to dissolve gelatin.
2. In a large heat resistant bowl beat whole egg and yolk with an electric mixer until thick and lemon colored, about 5 minutes. Add gelatin mixture and condensed milk. Beat on low speed just to mix.
3. In a medium saucepan bring 2 cups water to simmer over medium-high heat. Place the bowl with egg mixture over the saucepan, making sure the water does not touch the bottom of the bowl. Stir the egg mixture over simmering water until heated through and thickened, about 10 minutes. Do not let the egg mixture boil.
4. Remove the bowl from over the saucepan. Cool the mixture in the bowl. Refrigerate just until the mixture begins to set. Beat again with a mixer at high speed until light and creamy.
5. In a small bowl beat chilled evaporated skim milk with an electric mixer until doubled in volume. Fold into gelatin mixture with lime zest, if using, until thoroughly combined.
6. Pour the mixture into the pie crust. Refrigerate until set.
7. Meringues:
- Preheat the oven to 275°F.
- Cover a baking sheet with wax paper or parchment paper.
- In a medium bowl beat egg white and salt until stiff peaks form.
- Beat in 3 tablespoons of sugar gradually until very stiff.
- Fold in the remaining tablespoon of sugar and vanilla.
- Spoon the meringue mixture into a sealable plastic bag. Seal it.
- Snip about 1/4 inch diagonally from one bottom corner. Gently squeeze the bag over the prepared baking sheet to force out small dollops of meringue, 3/4 inch in diameter. Make dollops until the mixture is all used, about 10.
8. Bake the meringues for 40 minutes until dry to touch and lightly colored. With a spatula lift the meringues from paper while still warm. Store in an airtight container at room temperature or freeze tightly in a sealed freezer bag. Use as desired.
9. To serve, arrange lime slices around the pie. Top each lime slice with a meringue.
Crumb Pie Crust:
- In a small bowl or food processor, thoroughly mix 1 cup low-fat graham-cracker crumbs and 1/4 teaspoon ground cinnamon. Mix in 3 tablespoons softened light butter until well incorporated.
- Coat a 9-inch pie plate with nonstick butter-flavored cooking spray. Press the crumb mixture over the bottom and up the side of the plate. Bake in a 375°F oven for about 8 minutes or until firm and lightly colored.
